plan: Eliminate all highly leveraged ETFs to reduce risk.
Significantly trim semiconductor exposure to lower concentration.
Increase allocation to income-generating assets like QQQI.
Reduce broad index holdings to free up capital.
Add a top-rated software name like PANW for quality.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer for stability.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target monthly return around 1.5%. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Slightly reduce exposure to leveraged ETFs like SOXL.
Trim semiconductor holdings to manage sector risk.
Reduce concentration in broad index ETFs (SPY, QQQ).
Add two high-quality software names (PANW, DDOG) for growth.
Rebalance the portfolio towards a more diversified mix.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly return by reducing leverage and concentration while adding quality growth names. It aims for risk-first growth with moderate drawdowns.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged semiconductor ETF (SOXL).
Add to semiconductor holdings (SMH) for sector concentration.
Reduce broad index exposure (SPY, QQQ) to fund adds.
Increase position in top performer (CRWD).
Add two high-scoring scanner names (META, PANW).
Maintain a small cash buffer to maximize leverage.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly return by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-scoring tech and semiconductor names. Expect higher potential gains and significantly larger drawdowns due to leverage and daily rebalancing of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate volatility.
Sell high-beta single names like CRWD and SMH.
Significantly increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI).
Increase allocation to broad index ETF (SPY) for income.
Focus on holdings that provide regular monthly distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and potential reinvestment.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly return through distributions from income-generating ETFs and broad market exposure. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, with NAV still subject to market declines.
